answer:
The Ya-yue Shang mode with ♭B as the Zhi note corresponds to a different transposition than when ♭B is the tonic. In Chinese pentatonic theory, the Zhi note is the fifth degree, so ♭B as Zhi implies the tonic is ♭E, which has three flats, while ♭B as tonic has two flats. Therefore, the former has one more flat sign than the latter, making the correct answer that the latter has one more flat sign than the former.

\boxed{C}
